description: XXL Lysimeter measurement data (Carbon Drawdown Initiative)
generated_from: AIR lab reports (CDIFUE21_AB*.xlsx) + logbook + PN protocols
days_definition: days = date - 2022-05-15 (experiment start for soil Fürth 2022).

volume_variants.volume_pumped: own pumped volume at sampling (XLSX Bemerkung, else PN-PDF/OCR)
volume_variants.volume_avgBD: old approach: A-pots = mean of parallel B/C/D pots
volume_variants.volume_dumpmodel: pumped + logbook dumps since previous sampling

below_lod_convention: values '<x' set to 0 (matches notebook); flagged in long format
scope_note: Public release: soil 'Fürth 2022' experiment only (started 2022-05-15). A 2025 follow-on experiment (most pots repurposed with a new soil) is separate and not yet released.
export_is_gross_not_input_corrected: TA export = leachate concentration x drainage volume; NOT netted against alkalinity carried in by irrigation water. In 2022 each pot received ~111 L hard municipal tap water (Infra Fuerth, 16.7 dGH, ~5.1 mmol/L HCO3 by ion balance) vs ~160 L rain; that input is of order 0.5 tCO2e/ha (up to ~0.6 as a strict mass-balance ceiling), ~a third of the ~1.6 gross cumulative. So absolute CDR is an upper bound (irrigation-corrected ~1.0-1.1 tCO2e/ha, less after downstream losses). Common-mode across pots, so treatment-vs-control comparisons are unaffected. First ~90 days = initial flush / Birch effect, not weathering.
